  "sound_source": "water hammer",

  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Severe water hammer (hydraulic shock) occurs immediately after the fixture valve closes.",
    "Lack of adequate piping support, securing, or water hammer arrestors."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Install an ASSE 1010 certified water hammer arrestor close to the fast-closing valve.",
    "Secure loose PEX and copper piping with approved hangers and straps to prevent movement."
  ],
  "rationale": "The audio recording clearly captures a valve closing followed by a loud, resonant metallic banging sound characteristic of water hammer. This hydraulic shock can damage joints, fittings, and appliances over time, violating standard plumbing codes regarding piping support and pressure surge control.",
  "references": [
    "IPC Section 604.9 (Water hammer)",
    "UPC Section 609.10 (Water Hammer Arrestors)"
  ]
}
